member.name: T:Calendar.CalendarFiles
member.summary: CalendarFiles class is used to manage the file paths used used in the Calendar project

example.code:
CalenderFiles File = New CalenderFiles()
String FilePath = ".\Users\TestUser\AppData\Local\Calendar" String FileName = "calendar.db"
File.VerifyReadFromFileName(FilePath,FileName) File.VerifyWriteFromFileName(FilePath,FileName)

member.name: M:Calendar.CalendarFiles.VerifyReadFromFileName(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Verfies if the path to the file exists
member.param: Relative Path to the file
The Name of the file
member.exception: Throws when the filepath does not exist.

example.code:
String nullPath = null String fileName = "calendar.db"
CalenderFiles File = New CalenderFiles()
String returnPath = File.VerifyReadFromFileName(NullPath,FileName) Console.WriteLine(ReturnPath) = "%USERPROFILE%\\AppData\\Local\\" "Calendar\\" "Calendar.txt"
String fakePath = ".\This\Drive\Does\Not\Exist" returnPath = File.VerifyReadFromFileName(FakePath,fileName) returnPath = FileNotFoundException "ReadFromFileException: FilePath (" + FakePath + ") Does not exist"
string realPath = "".\Users\TestUser\AppData\Local\Calendar"" returnPath = File.VerifyReadFromFileName(realPath,fileName)
Console.WriteLine(realPath) = "".\Users\TestUser\AppData\Local\Calendar""

members.member:
Checks if the file is writable
Relative Path to the file Allows Nulls Name of the file Returns the filepath Throws when the specified directory isn't valid or The file isn't capable to be edited.
string nullPath = null string defaultFileName = calender.db
CalenderFiles File = New CalenderFiles()
Path = File.VeriyWriteToFileName(nullPath,defaultFileName) Console.WriteLine(Path) = "%USERPROFILE%\\AppData\\Local\\"
string fakePath = ".\This\Drive\Does\Not\Exist" Path = File.VerifyWriteToFileName() Path = Throw Exception ("SaveToFileException: FilePath("%USERPROFILE%\\AppData\\Local\\")
string realPath = "".\Users\TestUser\AppData\Local\Calendar"" returnPath = File.VerifyWriteFromFileName(realPath,fileName)
Console.WriteLine(realPath) = "".\Users\TestUser\AppData\Local\Calendar""
Thrown when the directory does not exist or the file is read-only. Thrown when the provided file path is null. Thrown when the caller does not have the required permission. Thrown when the specified path is invalid. Thrown when an I/O error occurs. Thrown when one of the arguments provided to a method is not valid. Thrown when the specified path, file name, or both exceed the system-defined maximum length. Thrown when the path is in an invalid format. Thrown when the caller does not have the required permission. "

member.name: T:Calendar.CalendarItem
member.summary: Represents a single scheduled Calender entry in the Calender entry.

example.code: CalendarItem = new CalendarItem(2,2, new DateTime(2026,1,26),"Fun","Went to the beach",73,73)

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.CategoryID
member.summary: Gets or Sets the CategoryId Number of a calendar entry.
member.value: An integer value that is used to determined an already predefined category.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.EventID
member.summary: Gets or Sets the EventId number of a calendar entry
member.value: An integer value that is used to an already determined an already predefined event.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.StartDateTime
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Date of a calendar entry.
member.value: The date and time when the event started in DD/MM/YYYY

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.Category
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Category of a calendar entry.
member.value: A string of the chosen Category like Holiday,Work

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.ShortDescription
member.summary: Gets or sets a short description of a calendar entry.
member.value: a brief description of the event that occured.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.DurationInMinutes
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Duration of the event in minutes of a calendar entry.
member.value: The lenght of how long the event lasted measured in minutes.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItem.BusyTime
member.summary: The Duration of the event in minutes of a calendar entry.
member.value: The lenght of how long the event lasted measured in minutes.

member.name: T:Calendar.CalendarItemsByMonth
member.summary: Represents The total Events and busy time that took place in a month.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByMonth.Month
member.summary: Get or sets The Month that the event took place in.
member.value: A month out of the twelve months in the year.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByMonth.Items
member.summary: Gets or Sets A list of Events that took place in the month
member.value: A List of Calendar Items

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByMonth.TotalBusyTime
member.summary: The Accumulation of time that all of the events took
member.value: A double value that measures the total time all of the events took combined.

member.name: T:Calendar.CalendarItemsByCategory
member.summary: Groups the Calendar Items By Category.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByCategory.Category
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Category to filter and group the events.
member.value: a string of the Type of event that took place

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByCategory.Items
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Filtered Items by category.
member.value: A list of items grouped by category.

member.name: P:Calendar.CalendarItemsByCategory.TotalBusyTime
member.summary: Gets or Sets the total BusyTime that the group event accumulated.
member.value: A double value that measures the total time all of the events took combined

member.name: T:Calendar.Categories
member.summary: Manages the collection of Category objects stored in the SQLite database Provides functionality to add, delete, update, and retrieve categories
member.remarks: This class interacts directly with the SQLite database using the provided database connection

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.#ctor(System.Data.SQLite.SQLiteConnection,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Categories class.
member.param: An open SQLite database connection.
Indicates whether the database is newly created.

example.code:
SQLiteConnection connection = Database.dbConnection;
Categories categories = new Categories(connection, true);

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.GetCategoryFromId(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves a category using its ID.
member.param: The category ID.

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.SetCategoriesToDefaults
member.summary: Clears existing category tables and inserts the default categories.

example.code:
Categories categories = new Categories(Database.dbConnection, false);
categories.SetCategoriesToDefaults();

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.Add(System.String)
member.summary: Adds a new category type
member.param: The description of the category type.

example.code:
Categories categories = new Categories(Database.dbConnection, false);
categories.Add("Custom Type");

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.Add(System.String,Calendar.Category.CategoryType)
member.summary: Adds a new category
member.param: The category description.
The category type.

example.code:
Categories categories = new Categories(Database.dbConnection, false);
categories.Add("Gym", Category.CategoryType.Event);

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.Delete(System.Int32)
member.summary: Deletes a category and its related events.
member.param: The ID of the category to delete.

example.code:
Categories categories = new Categories(Database.dbConnection, false);
categories.Delete(3);

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.List
member.summary: Returns all categories in the database.

member.name: M:Calendar.Categories.UpdateProperties(System.Int32,System.String,Calendar.Category.CategoryType)
member.summary: Updates the description and type of a category.
member.param: The category ID.
The new description.
The new category type.

example.code:
Categories categories = new Categories(Database.dbConnection, false);
categories.UpdateProperties(2, "Updated Category", Category.CategoryType.Event);

member.name: T:Calendar.Category
member.summary: Creates Categories for calendar Items

example.code:
Category cat = new Category(12, "Non Standard", Category.CategoryType.Event);
Console.Writeline(cat.ToString)

member.name: P:Calendar.Category.Id
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Id for a single category entry.
member.value: The integer value that the category holds

member.name: P:Calendar.Category.Description
member.summary: Gets or Sets the description for a single category entry
member.value: a string representation of the description for the category.

member.name: P:Calendar.Category.Type
member.summary: Represents the types of Category thats can exist

member.name: F:Calendar.Category.CategoryType.Event
member.summary: Event represents just a normal event like going outside

member.name: F:Calendar.Category.CategoryType.AllDayEvent
member.summary: AllDayEvent represents an event that takes up the entire day

member.name: F:Calendar.Category.CategoryType.Holiday
member.summary: Holiday represents an event that is a holiday

member.name: F:Calendar.Category.CategoryType.Availability
member.summary: Availibility represents when the event is free time.

member.name: M:Calendar.Category.#ctor(System.Int32,System.String,Calendar.Category.CategoryType)
member.summary: Creates an instance of Category.
member.param: The Id of the Category
A Short Description of the category
The Type that the event associates with.

example.code:
Category = new Category(1,"work stuff",Event)
Category.Id = 1 Category.Description = "Work stuff" Category.Type = Event

member.name: M:Calendar.Category.#ctor(Calendar.Category)
member.summary: Creates a Copy of the most recent Instance of a Category
member.param: An old instance of Category.

example.code:
Category = new Category(1,"work stuff",Event)
Category.Id = 1 Category.Description = "Work stuff" Category.Type = Event
Category = new Category2(Category)
Category12.Id = 1 Category2.Description = "Work stuff" Category2.Type = Event

member.name: M:Calendar.Category.ToString
member.summary: Creates an Easy to read String for Reusabiltiy.
member.returns: Description: A Description of the Category

example.code:
Category = new Category(1,"work stuff",Event)
Console.WriteLine(Category.Tostring())
Output "Work Stuff"

member.name: T:Calendar.Database
member.summary: Provides methods for creating, opening, and managing the SQLite database used by the Calendar application
member.remarks: This class handles database creation, table setup, connections, and closing of the database. It makes sure all tables (events, categories, categoryTypes) are correctly set up and connected.

member.name: P:Calendar.Database.dbConnection
member.summary: Gets the active SQLite database connection
member.value: The currently open SQLiteConnection object.

member.name: M:Calendar.Database.newDatabase(System.String)
member.summary: Creates a new SQLite database with the given filename, sets up the tables, and opens the connection.
member.param: The file path of the new SQLite database.

example.code: Database.newDatabase("calendar.db"); SQLiteConnection conn = Database.dbConnection;

member.name: M:Calendar.Database.existingDatabase(System.String)
member.summary: Opens an existing SQLite database file.
member.param: The file path of the existing SQLite database.

example.code: Database.existingDatabase("calendar.db"); SQLiteConnection conn = Database.dbConnection;

member.name: M:Calendar.Database.CloseDatabaseAndReleaseFile
member.summary: Safely closes the database connection and releases any resources.

example.code: Database.CloseDatabaseAndReleaseFile();

member.name: M:Calendar.Database.connectDB(System.String)
member.summary: Connects to a SQLite database file and opens the connection.
member.param: The file path of the SQLite database to connect to.

example.code: Database.connectDB("calendar.db"); SQLiteConnection conn = Database.dbConnection;

member.name: M:Calendar.Database.CreateTable(System.String)
member.summary: Creates the tables (events, categories, categoryTypes) in the SQLite database it also drops existing tables if they exist.
member.param: The filename or connection string (currently not used because the connection uses static _connection).

example.code: Database.CreateTable("calendar.db");

member.name: P:Calendar.Event.Id
member.summary: Gets The Id Assigned to the event and returns it.
member.value: Stores an Id which is an integer value.

member.name: P:Calendar.Event.StartDateTime
member.summary: Gets The Start Date And Time When the event occured
member.value: Stores The Date In YYYY/MM/DD Format

member.name: P:Calendar.Event.DurationInMinutes
member.summary: Gets Or Sets the duration Of the Event measured in minutes.
member.value: Stores the Duration of the event in minutes.

member.name: P:Calendar.Event.Details
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Description/Details of the event
member.value: Stores the Description/Details of the event as a a string

member.name: P:Calendar.Event.Category
member.summary: Gets or Sets the Category as an integer number which associates to an already predefined category.
member.value: Stores an integer value which represents a category.

member.name: M:Calendar.Event.#ctor(System.Int32,System.DateTime,System.Int32,System.Double,System.String)
member.summary: Creates an Instance of Event
member.param: The Id of the event
The Date the event Occured in YYYY/MM/DD Format
The Category the Event is classified as.
The Duration of the Event measured in minutes.
The Details/Description of the event

example.code:
Event Event = New Event(1,New DateTime(2026,2,27),90,"Went to the WaterPark",5)
Event.Id = 1 Event.StartDateTime = "2026,2,27" Event.DurationInMinutes = 90 Event.Details = "Went to the WaterPark

member.name: M:Calendar.Event.#ctor(Calendar.Event)
member.summary: Creates An Copy of an instance depending on the passed in Object.
member.param: The object to Copy

example.code:
Event Event = New Event(1,New DateTime(2026,2,27),90,"Went to the WaterPark",5)
Event CopyEvent(Event)
CopyEvent.Id = 1 CopyEvent.StartDateTime = "2026,2,27" CopyEvent.DurationInMinutes = 90 CopyEvent.Details = "Went to the WaterPark"

member.name: T:Calendar.Events
member.summary: Manages the collection of Event objects, providing methods to retrieve, add, delete, and update events from a database.

member.name: F:Calendar.Events._connection
member.summary: The SQLite database connection used to do all operations.

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.#ctor(System.Data.SQLite.SQLiteConnection,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates an instance of Events connected to the database.
member.param: The connection to the database.
If true, a new database is initiliazed. Else, the existing one is used.

example.code: Events events = new Events(connection, false);

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.List
member.summary: Retrieves all events in the database as a list of Event objects.
member.returns: List of Event objects in the database.

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.Add(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds a new Event object to the database.
member.param: The int ID of the category of the event.
The duration of the event in minutes.
The start date and time of the event.
The description/details of the event.

example.code:
Events events = new Events(connection, false);
events.Add(1, 90, "2026-03-25", "basketball session")

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.Delete(System.Int32)
member.summary: Deletes an event with the specified ID from the database.
member.param: The int ID of the event to delete.

example.code:
Events events = new Events(connection, false);
events.Delete(1);

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.UpdateEvent(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Double,System.DateTime,System.String)
member.summary: Updates an existing event with new values.
member.param: The ID of the event to delete.
The new category ID for the event.
The new duration of the event.
The new start date time of the event.
The new description/details of the event.

example.code:
Events events = new Events(connection, false);
events.UpdateEvent(1, 2, 360, new DateTime(2026, 3, 27), "basketball tournament");

member.name: M:Calendar.Events.GetEventFromId(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves an event from its ID.
member.param: The ID of the event to retrieve.
member.returns: The Event object matching the ID.
member.exception: Thrown when no event with the ID exists in the database.

member.name: T:Calendar.HomeCalendar
member.summary: Creates a calendar (dictionary) of events grouped by categories. Categories and events are now loaded directly from the database.

member.name: P:Calendar.HomeCalendar.categories
member.summary: Gets the list of categories
member.value: stores a list of category object

member.name: P:Calendar.HomeCalendar.events
member.summary: Gets a list of events
member.value: stores a list of event objects

member.name: M:Calendar.HomeCalendar.GetCalendarItems(System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves all calendar items directly from the database, filters by date range and optionally by category
member.param: The start date. Defaults to 1/1/1900 if null.
The end date. Defaults to 1/1/2500 if null.
If true, filters by the specified CategoryID.
The CategoryID to filter by if FilterFlag is true.
member.returns: Returns a list of CalendarItem objects with calculated BusyTime

member.name: M:Calendar.HomeCalendar.GetCalendarItemsByMonth(System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves calendar items grouped by month using database queries. The distinct months are first queried from the database, then items for each month are retrieved by calling GetCalendarItems() with the appropriate month range.
member.param: The start date. Defaults to 1/1/1900 if null.
The end date. Defaults to 1/1/2500 if null.
If true, filters by the specified CategoryID.
The CategoryID to filter by if FilterFlag is true.
member.returns: Returns a list of CalendarItemsByMonth, each containing a month string, items list, and total busy time.

member.name: M:Calendar.HomeCalendar.GetCalendarItemsByCategory(System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves calendar items grouped by category using database queries. Distinct categories in the date range are first queried from the database, then GetCalendarItems() is called for each category to get the detailed items.
member.param: The start date. Defaults to 1/1/1900 if null.
The end date. Defaults to 1/1/2500 if null.
If true, filters by the specified CategoryID.
The CategoryID to filter by if FilterFlag is true.
member.returns: Returns a list of CalendarItemsByCategory, each containing a category name, items list, and total busy time.

member.name: M:Calendar.HomeCalendar.GetCalendarDictionaryByCategoryAndMonth(System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.Boolean,System.Int32)
member.summary: Gets a list of dictionary by Grouping all events by Category and Month one per month and one per category and returns a summary
member.param: The Start Date by YYYY/MM/DD.
The End Date by YYYY/MM/DD
if true filter the Calendar items by category.
The Category to be filter by
